cxu_object.claim: Trailing stops must tighten dynamically as unrealized profit grows to protect captured gains — start at 1.5% trail, tighten to 0.75% once unrealized profit exceeds $200, and tighten further to 0.5% once unrealized profit exceeds $400. This ratcheting mechanic prevents giving back large open profits on reversals. Additionally, if a position is stopped out but the original thesis remains valid (regime unchanged, signal consensus unchanged), re-entering the same direction wastes fees and resets the cost basis unfavorably — in this case it is better to widen the stop pre-emptively and hold rather than accept a stop-out and pay re-entry fees. The ratchet only moves in one direction: stops tighten as profit grows and never loosen once tightened. Observed instance: long position accumulated $500+ unrealized profit in volatile regime, 1.5% trailing stop was too loose, price reversed and gave back most of the move. Had the stop ratcheted to 0.5% at the $400 profit threshold, a significant portion of the gain would have been locked in.

supporting_contexts.text: Human observation at 2026-04-10T16:00:44.202574+00:00: Had a long position that ran up $500+ in unrealized profit but the 1.5% trailing stop was too loose to capture the gains. Price reversed and gave back most of the move. The trailing stop should tighten dynamically as profit grows — for example, start at 1.5% but tighten to 0.75% once profit exceeds $200, and to 0.5% once profit exceeds $400. This is a ratcheting ratchet — the trail itself gets tighter as the position becomes more profitable. Also, re-entering the same trade direction after a stop-out wastes fees — if the thesis hasnt changed, it is better to widen the stop and hold rather than get stopped out and re-enter.. Market context: volatile regime, $2223.75, BB=21%, trend=-1.0423%, position=FLAT.
